channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇰🇵 Kim Jong Un Praises North Korean Soldier Suicides in Ukraine War
item.description: At a ceremony unveiling a bronze sculpture of North Korean and Russian soldiers, Kim Jong Un explicitly praised troops who chose death over capture in Ukraine, the most direct acknowledgment yet of Pyongyang battlefield doctrine.

item.title: 🛢️ Oil Surges Past $126, Highest in Four Years on Hormuz Fears
item.description: Brent crude briefly touched $126 per barrel, the highest price in four years, as traders worry about prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz following Trump decision to extend Iran port blockade.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Russia Scales Back Victory Day Parade Amid Ukrainian Drone Threats
item.description: Moscow is reducing the footprint of its foremost annual military parade as Ukraine drone attacks inside Russia expand. The scaled-back May 9 celebration reflects the growing security challenges.

item.title: 🇩🇪 Trump Threatens US Troop Cuts in Germany Over Iran War Criticism
item.description: President Trump threatened to reduce US military presence in Germany after Chancellor Merz criticized US handling of the Iran war. Germany hosts over 35,000 US troops and is key to NATO operations.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US Seeks International Coalition to Reopen Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The State Department is pressing for an international coalition to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, according to a cable seen by Reuters, as oil prices surge on fears of prolonged supply disruption.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Russian Shadow Airlines Use Algeria as Base for Secretive Military Missions
item.description: Algeria has become a key hub for Russian military logistics as shadow airlines conduct covert missions, raising concerns about Moscow's expanding influence in North Africa.

item.title: 🛢️ Countries Pay Iran Transit Fees to Pass Through Strait of Hormuz Despite US Sanctions
item.description: China, India, Japan and other nations continue paying Iran transit fees to ship oil through the Strait of Hormuz, circumventing US sanctions and providing Tehran with revenue.

item.title: ⚔️ US Eyes First Combat Deployment of Hypersonic Missile Against Iran
item.description: The US military is considering deploying hypersonic missiles against Iranian targets, which would mark the first combat use of the weapons system that has not been declared fully operational.

item.title: 🇪🇺 Eurozone Economic Growth Drops to Near Zero as Energy Costs Surge
item.description: The eurozone economy barely grew in the first quarter of 2026 as soaring energy costs from the Middle East war took their toll on European industry and consumer spending.

item.title: 🛢️ Russia Dismisses Price War Fears After UAE OPEC Exit
item.description: Russia's Deputy PM Novak said OPEC+ will continue despite UAE departure, with no price war expected. The cartel faces its biggest shakeup in years as UAE exits to pump more oil.

item.title: 🏛️ Supreme Court Hollows Out Voting Rights Act Protections
item.description: The Supreme Court ruled Louisiana's 2024 election map an unconstitutional racial gerrymander, dealing a major blow to the Voting Rights Act and minority voting rights protections.

item.title: 💰 US War in Iran Has Cost $25 Billion So Far, Pentagon Official Says
item.description: A senior Pentagon official provided the first official estimate of the military's price tag for the Iran conflict, with $25 billion spent so far ahead of midterm elections.

item.title: 🇱🇧 More Than 1 Million in Lebanon Face Acute Food Insecurity
item.description: A hunger monitoring group reports over 1 million people in Lebanon are expected to face acute food insecurity as the Middle East conflict disrupts supply chains and drives up prices.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK Antisemitism Stabbing Suspect Known to Counter-Radicalization Program
item.description: British police said the man suspected of stabbing two Jewish men was known to the government's counter-radicalization program, amid warnings of state-sponsored threats.

item.title: ✈️ First US Commercial Flight to Venezuela in Years
item.description: A commercial flight from the US to Venezuela landed in Caracas for the first time in years, marking a potential shift in US-Venezuela relations amid changing geopolitical dynamics.

item.title: 📰 Media Groups Call on Israel to Allow Gaza Press Access
item.description: Major media organizations are urging Israel to lift a ban preventing foreign journalists from independently entering Gaza, a restriction in place since October 2023.

item.title: 📊 Powell Reflects on Fed as Inflation Pressures Mount
item.description: Fed Chair Jerome Powell reflected on his tenure as inflation expectations rise again, with oil prices surging from the Iran conflict putting pressure on the US economy.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine Orders Mandatory Front-Line Rotation After 2 Months
item.description: Ukraine's military chief Syrskyi ordered mandatory rotation of soldiers from front-line positions after 2 months of continuous combat to address fatigue and morale.

item.title: ✈️ Middle East Airlines See 60% Drop in Demand in March
item.description: Airlines in the Middle East experienced a 60% drop in passenger demand in March as the US-Iran conflict disrupted regional travel and tourism.

item.title: 🇵🇰 Pakistan Opens Land Routes to Iran Despite US Pressure
item.description: Pakistan has opened land routes to Iran for trade and transit, complicating its relationship with the US as Washington pressures allies to isolate Tehran.

item.title: 🛢️ Wall Street Giants Circle Shell's $15 Billion LNG Canada Stake
item.description: Major Wall Street firms are circling Shell's $15 billion stake in LNG Canada as the energy project moves toward completion amid global energy market disruptions.

item.title: 🇬🇧 King Charles Addresses Congress During US State Visit
item.description: King Charles III addressed Congress during his state visit to the US, emphasizing the strength of the US-UK relationship at a time of global challenges.

item.title: 🇬🇧 King Charles Highlights US-UK Bond in White House Speech
item.description: King Charles highlighted the enduring US-UK relationship in a speech at the White House state dinner, addressing the importance of the alliance amid global uncertainty.

item.title: 🇲🇽 Mexican Drug Bust Reveals Sinaloa Cartel Operations
item.description: Mexican authorities seized significant drug shipments and revealed new details about Sinaloa cartel operations amid ongoing efforts to combat drug trafficking.

item.title: 🛢️ Oil Prices Extend Gains as US-Iran Deadlock Keeps Supply Off Market
item.description: Oil prices continued to rise as the US-Iran deadlock kept supplies off the market, with the Strait of Hormuz closure affecting global energy flows.
